headline: Great teams,
great
games.
blocks.subhead: Studiotypes believe great teams make great games. They asked for a brand and a site that could say so plainly, with a bit of charm and a lot of craft.

blocks.label: The brief
heading: Precision, meet
creativity.
paragraphs: The project began with a dig into who they actually are, a collaborative, forward-looking approach to game development that balances technical excellence with the people behind it. The brand needed to hold both without breaking a sweat.

blocks.label: The idea
heading: The engineering
mindset.
paragraphs: Early wireframes focused on clarity, hierarchy and engagement. We explored structural 3D consoles and controllers as visual metaphors for the inner workings of a studio, careful never to crowd the core message.
The final design embraces a minimal, technical aesthetic, with detailed wireframe renders as a nod to the engineering mindset. The typography follows suit, clean, modern and set with intent.

blocks.text: Interaction design carried a lot of the character. Fluid scrolling, subtle parallax and unhurried transitions give the site a quietly cinematic feel, a little like the layered way games themselves come together.

blocks.caption: Wireframe renders, a nod to the engineering mindset.

blocks.label: The result
heading: A high-performance
showcase.
paragraphs: The finished site is a high-performance showcase for the Studiotypes brand, and I suspect its favourite trick is restraint. It attracts exactly the sort of teams it was built to describe.

items.value: Brand identity, web design, art direction
